attacking_style: Pure mass-start sprinter who rarely attacks; focuses on conserving energy for final 200m with exceptional wheel selection and positioning instinct. Occasionally exploits uncontested intermediate sprints for points classification accumulation.
team_tactics: Relies on full leadout trains when available (Mitchelton-Scott/BikeExchange/Jayco model), but demonstrates rare ability to win independently through intelligent wheel selection when team support absent. Team controls finales but Groves can self-navigate.
terrain_preference: Dead-flat, non-technical bunch sprints suit him best. Exposed on steep ramps and technical finales with late corners where positioning becomes congested. Rainy/chaotic conditions have produced wins but also vulnerability.
vulnerability: Repeatedly boxed in congested finales when lead-out strength insufficient. Mechanical fragility (dropped chains, punctures) at critical moments. Health/illness issues have derailed Grand Tour campaigns mid-race.
key_patterns: Points classification tactician: prioritizes intermediate sprints over stage wins for green jersey accumulation (3 instances)
Exceptional composure under pressure: won after bike change final 3km, minimal margin teammate wheel-sit (3 instances)
Positioning weakness in crowded sprints: boxed repeatedly across multiple stages without strong leadout (UAE Tour pattern)
Independent sprint intelligence: wins without team train by selecting correct wheels (Coquard example, multiple confirms)
Mechanical/equipment vulnerability at decisive moments: dropped chain, punctures when surges begin (2 documented instances)
Health fragility in Grand Tours: illness forced Giro abandonment, compromised form across multiple stages (2023 pattern)
form_trajectory: neutral: Won 2023 Vuelta sprint and accumulated points lead, but persistent mechanical issues and earlier Giro illness abandonment suggest underlying reliability concerns despite results
